message:
bench(fj-dispatch): add F32 scalar scan/while vmap baselines (frankenjax-l3zw)

dispatch_baseline currently exercises I64 and F64 literal lanes in
the scan/while vmap benchmarks but skips native F32. Per
frankenjax-l3zw, BatchTrace scalar scan/while fast paths handle I64
and F64 literals directly while native F32Bits scalar lanes fall
back to generic per-element control-flow batching — so we need
before/after baselines to prove the planned F32 fast path actually
moves the number, and to catch parity regressions.

Add two F32-specific benchmarks while keeping every existing case
untouched:

bench_vmap_scan
- shared_init_batched_xs_f32_add_128x64: scan_control_flow_jaxpr("add")
  vmapped with a shared scalar f32 init (0.5) and a 128x64 F32 xs
  tensor populated with 1.0 + (idx % 17) * 0.25 so the inputs
  exercise non-trivial F32 bit patterns rather than zeros.
  vmap_in_axes is set to "none,0" to match the prior shared-init /
  batched-xs convention used by the I64/F64 cases above.

bench_vmap_while
- batched_scalar_f32_add_128: vmaps the existing while jaxpr with
  three rank-1 F32 vectors of length 128 — init in [0,8), step in
  [1,5), threshold in [96, 112). The threshold deltas guarantee
  loops actually iterate (not all converge on step 0), so the
  scalar-loop fast path is genuinely exercised.

Construction failures bubble up via .expect() in the bench setup
(criterion harness convention here), and the dispatch step likewise
expects success so a regression to control-flow batching surfaces as
a panic rather than a silent miscount. Beads frankenjax-l3zw is
flipped to in_progress to track the parity/perf work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
